title: Search freshness + late discovery detection + applicant tracking
description:
Four changes to prevent missing roles like Strava EM Identity (170 applicants at discovery):

1. TIGHTEN hours_old: Use hours_old=4 during frequent runs (7am-7pm). Keep hours_old=24 for overnight/weekend as safety net. Reduces seen-job noise that pushes fresh roles out of the 100-result window.

2. TARGETED DEEP SEARCH: Add daily search for domain-specific terms ('EM identity', 'EM authentication', 'EM platform security') with hours_old=72. Catches niche roles that rank low in generic searches.

3. APPLICANT COUNT UPDATES: After processing new jobs, re-fetch page for top N high-rated seen jobs (Fit 7+, <100 applicants, not applied) and update applicant_count. No Claude call needed.

4. LATE DISCOVERY FLAG: If a newly-discovered job has >25 applicants, log warning. If >50, send Discord alert with 'LATE DISCOVERY' flag for investigation.

Also: gather and report stats on seen vs new per run (how many of the 100 results were already seen vs fresh).
notes:
Revised spec (2026-05-02):

hours_old strategy:
- Set hours_old = 1.5 × interval_since_last_run (not a fixed value)
- Log jobs returned per run
- If that window returns >60 jobs: increase run frequency for that time-of-day slot
- If numerous new discoveries have >25 (or >50) applicants: also increase frequency
- Floor: never more frequent than every 30 minutes
- Ideal steady-state: hours_old ≈ time since last run, window returns <60 jobs

Adaptive frequency model:
- Track run history by time-of-day
- Learn which slots need higher frequency (busy posting windows)
- Adjust schedule dynamically rather than hardcoding daytime/overnight splits

Supersedes the static hours_old=4 / hours_old=24 approach in original description.
Items 2 (domain search) and 4 (late discovery flag) from original spec still apply.
Item 3 (applicant count refresh) overlaps with ja-d1l — coordinate before implementing.

title: Deterministic scoring engine
description: Port RAS scoring framework into code. Config-driven weighted scoring: role type, domain, company tier, location, compensation. No LLM. Score threshold gates Claude calls. Start threshold conservatively low, log both code-score and Claude-score to calibrate.

title: Deterministic filter pipeline
description: Replace Claude relevance check with deterministic filters for: experience-years regex extraction, location string matching, industry/title keyword matching from filters.yaml, salary floor from config. Only edge cases go to Claude.

title: Config schema + LLM drafting CLI
description: Define profile.yaml, search.yaml, scoring.yaml, filters.yaml schemas. Build CLI: python setup_config.py --resume path/to/resume. LLM reads resume, drafts all 4 configs, user reviews/edits. Port scoring framework from RAS job_factor_lookups.xlsx.

title: Update Act/Pri scoring to use lib_score + haiku_score correctly after RAS split
description:
RAS split haiku_score (Claude Haiku LLM, 0-10) from lib_score (deterministic lib scorer, 0-100). Both columns now exist in Supabase. The computePriority() formula in app/jobs/page.tsx and any other consumers need updating.

Current broken fallback:
  suit = ras_interest ?? (haiku_score * 10)  // assumes haiku_score is 0-10

Correct fallback chain (most → least authoritative):
  suit = ras_interest ?? lib_score ?? (haiku_score * 10)

Also need:
- Add lib_score to Job type in lib/supabase.ts
- Audit computePriority() merit formula — ras_fit (1-8 scale) and lib_score (0-100) have different ranges, formula should handle each independently
- Handle all combinations gracefully: only ras_interest, only lib_score, only haiku_score, any combo missing
- Check scraper post_to_job_agent() — does it now send lib_score separately? If so, ingest route needs to accept it
- Check supabase_jobs.py --backfill — currently writes haiku_score=lib_score_value (0-100 into 0-10 field). Fix to write lib_score instead.
- Any other consumers: Discord alerts, RAS scoring scripts, search-results CSVs
close_reason: lib_score added to Job type, computePriority fallback chain, ingest route, and scraper payload

title: One-time backfill search: 2-week deep scan
description: Run a one-time deep scan to find roles missed due to the 100-result cap over the past 2 weeks. Use results_wanted=500 or pagination if JobSpy supports it. Use the consolidated boolean searches with hours_old=336 (14 days). Most results will be seen (deduped), but the long tail beyond position 100 may contain missed high-value roles. Run once, then analyze what was found beyond the normal window.

title: Urgency-based alerting for actionable jobs
description: Jobs passing both relevance and fit thresholds get urgency-tiered alerts based on freshness + applicant count. Model: URGENT (first hour + <10 applicants, strong Discord ping), HIGH (1-4h or 10-25 applicants, normal alert), MEDIUM (4-24h or 25-50, quiet), LOW (>24h + >50, dashboard only). Key principle: if you'd accept it and could succeed at it, optimize chance of getting it. Replaces the current flat 2h/10-applicant passes_or_filter. Steep decay from URGENT to HIGH, slower aging curve after that.

title: Commute-aware location filtering
description:
Replace 3-city queries with single radius from Orinda (done). Add commute-aware filtering:

COMMUTE MODEL (effective weekly one-way minutes):
- Remote day penalties (cumulative): 1d=10m, 2d=35m, 3d=60m, 4d=105m, 5d=150m
- Weekly effective = remote_penalty[remote_days] + (in_person_days × one_way_commute_minutes)
- Assume 3 days in-office unless posting is explicit about schedule
- Fully remote: 150m/wk effective. 5d in-person 20min commute: 100m/wk. User prefers in-person.

LOCATION:
- Precomputed commute times from Piedmont for known cities
- Unknown cities: estimate from nearest precomputed city + lat/lng adjustment
- Shuttle companies: commute time becomes usable work time (quality adjustment, not speed)

MULTI-LOCATION DEDUP:
- Same title+company at multiple locations: keep the one with lowest effective weekly commute
- Currently keeps first seen; needs commute calculation to pick best

title: Dismiss with reason (not hard delete)
description: All 'x' actions (Discord and dashboard) should dismiss with a reason, not hard delete. Dismissed jobs need to stay in the DB (marked as rejected with a reason) so: 1) we learn from dismissal patterns for future filtering, 2) the scraper doesn't re-ingest them as new. Dashboard X should prompt for a reason (like Discord does), set status=rejected, and store the reason. Discord dismiss already does this. Unify the behavior.
close_reason: Dismiss-with-reason replaces hard delete. Prompt, status=rejected, rejection_reason stored. Verified end-to-end.

title: Resume/CL generation refactor
description: Swap LaTeX for Typst (match RAS pipeline). User-agnostic templates driven by profile.yaml. Remove all Josh-specific content from tailor.py and templates.

title: Web UI + DB personalization
description: Remove hardcoded profile UUID from Supabase schema. System prompt reads from profile.yaml or DB. Remove Josh-specific seed data. Make web UI fully config-driven.

title: Claude relevance as calibrated fallback
description: Claude only fires for jobs above deterministic threshold. Prompt templates from config/prompts/relevance.yaml, not hardcoded. Log both code-score and Claude-score. Track false negative rate to gradually tighten threshold.

title: Scraper schedule + saved-jobs
description: Configurable schedule from schedule.yaml: 15min daytime (6am-8pm PT weekdays), 60min overnight/weekends. Add saved-jobs periodic check (less frequent). Cron generation from config.
close_reason: Scraper running via launchctl every 15 min. Schedule logic in run_scheduled.py: 7am-7pm every 15min, hourly evenings, overnight at 12:30/2/4/6am. Verified working.

title: Background thread: refresh applicant count + open/closed status per role
description: Add a code-only background thread (no Discord, no LLM) that periodically re-fetches each active job posting to update applicant_count and detect closed/filled postings. Track last_refreshed_at per job. Refresh frequency driven by priority signals: score (haiku/lib), status (Act/Pri), age of posting, and last known applicant count. High-scoring active jobs refresh more often; stale low-interest jobs refresh rarely. Separate from the main scrape loop — runs as a daemon thread or lightweight scheduler within the existing scraper process.

title: Enrich saved-jobs imports with scores and salary
description: supabase_jobs.py now auto-fetches job pages and runs lib scorer when importing bare saved-jobs CSVs. Added --backfill to patch existing bare records.
close_reason: implemented in supabase_jobs.py: auto-enrich on import + --backfill command

title: Posting HTML cache + similarity matching for repost detection
description: Cache full LinkedIn posting HTML by job ID (the scraper already fetches it in fetch_full_job_details). Store in SQLite or flat files keyed by LinkedIn job ID. Future: use cached HTML for similarity matching to detect jobs posted more than once with minor modifications — location changes, reposting with new URL, relaxed requirements, salary adjustments. Could use text similarity (TF-IDF, embeddings) on the cached content. Ties into ja-bs6 (repost URL detection) but goes deeper — detecting semantic reposts, not just URL duplicates.
close_reason: Posting HTML cache at ~/.cache/job-search/postings/{job_id}/. Cache hit/miss verified. 7-day TTL. Shared with RAS.

title: Detect reposted roles with modified URLs
description: LinkedIn sometimes reposts the same role with a different URL (new job ID). Detect these by matching on title+company combinations, or by fuzzy-matching descriptions. When detected, link to the original job record rather than creating a new one. This prevents duplicate kanban cards for the same role.

title: Connection cache with age-based refresh
description: Import LinkedIn connections from export or RAS extract_linkedin_connections.py output. Local lookup: do I know anyone at {company}? Age-based refresh policy. Enrich Discord alerts with connection info.

title: Config refactor epic
description: Externalize all user-specific config into yaml files. Make codebase user-agnostic. LLM drafts initial config from resume, then deterministic code handles filtering/scoring.

title: LinkedIn post monitoring for hiring signals
description: Add a scraping tier that monitors LinkedIn posts (not just job listings) for hiring signals — keywords like 'hiring', 'opening', 'looking for', 'join my team', 'we need', etc. Posts from connections and company pages are high-signal and low-competition (no formal applicant count). Could use authenticated session since volume is low. Complements the JobSpy discovery tier.

title: Sync pass: carry forward RAS tool improvements to job-agent
description: RAS has been independently evolving its tools (supabase_jobs.py, scoring, pipeline scripts, etc.) since the last coordinated sync. Need a pass to diff what RAS has changed, identify improvements worth carrying forward, and reconcile both sides. Focus areas: .claude/tools/, scout/scraper.py equivalents, scoring calibration, any new pipeline patterns.

title: Dynamic scrape interval based on yield
description: Adjust skip_scrape() to dynamically tune intervals based on recent run stats. Targets: desired number of new jobs per run, desired applicant count at discovery (lower = we're finding jobs earlier). Read from job_outcomes.csv and seen_jobs.db. If recent runs find 0 new jobs, widen interval. If avg applicant count at discovery is high, tighten. The skip_scrape() abstraction is already in place.

title: Evaluate loop/schedule skills for inbox polling
description: Investigate using /loop or /schedule skills for periodic inbox checking during idle sessions. Current approach relies on checking before each user response (every 3+ min). Gap: no checking during idle periods (user away). /loop could poll every 10 min while active. /schedule could run a lightweight cron that checks inboxes and notifies via Discord if messages are pending. Evaluate tradeoffs: token cost, complexity, actual need (how often do cross-project messages arrive?).
